name: Webprobe Reporter
description: Phase 4 report generation module that produces JSON and HTML output files from webprobe Run data. Generates comprehensive HTML reports with embedded CSS styling using Jinja2 templates, displaying site graph metrics, page details, security findings, broken links, auth violations, timing outliers, and exploration costs. Also exports raw JSON reports of the complete run data.

types.name: HTML_TEMPLATE
types.description: Jinja2 Template instance containing the full HTML report structure with embedded CSS. Uses GitHub dark theme styling and renders run metadata, analysis metrics, phase timing, page details, security findings, broken links, auth violations, and timing outliers.

functions.name: generate_report
functions.description: Phase 4: Generate JSON and/or HTML reports from a webprobe Run. Writes report.json (full run data dump) and/or report.html (styled visual report) to the specified run directory. Returns a PhaseStatus tracking execution timing and completion status.

inputs.name: run
inputs.description: The complete webprobe Run object containing graph, analysis, phases, and metadata to be reported

inputs.name: run_dir
inputs.description: Directory path where report files will be written (report.json and/or report.html)

inputs.name: formats
inputs.description: List of output formats to generate. Supports 'json' and 'html'. Defaults to both if None.

error_cases.name: FileWriteError
error_cases.condition: run_dir does not exist or is not writable

error_cases.name: SerializationError
error_cases.condition: run.model_dump_json() fails due to non-serializable data

error_cases.name: TemplateRenderError
error_cases.condition: HTML_TEMPLATE.render() fails due to missing attributes in run object or template syntax issues

error_cases.name: ImportError
error_cases.condition: webprobe.__version__ cannot be imported when generating HTML

preconditions: run_dir must be a valid Path object
run must be a valid Run instance with populated graph
formats if provided must contain only 'json' and/or 'html' strings
postconditions: Returns PhaseStatus with phase='report', status='completed', and duration_ms populated
If 'json' in formats: report.json file written to run_dir containing run.model_dump_json(indent=2)
If 'html' in formats: report.html file written to run_dir with rendered HTML template
PhaseStatus.started_at and completed_at are set to ISO format UTC timestamps
PhaseStatus.duration_ms contains elapsed time in milliseconds
side_effects: Writes report.json to filesystem if 'json' in formats
Writes report.html to filesystem if 'html' in formats
Imports webprobe.__version__ dynamically when HTML format is requested

structured_side_effects.description: JSON dump of complete Run data with 2-space indentation
Rendered HTML report from Jinja2 template with analysis visualization

invariants: HTML_TEMPLATE is a module-level constant Template instance initialized once
Default formats are ['json', 'html'] when formats parameter is None
PhaseStatus.status transitions from 'running' to 'completed' on success
Time measurements use time.monotonic() for duration and datetime.now(timezone.utc) for timestamps
HTML template expects specific Run model structure with graph.nodes, analysis, phases, and explore_cost attributes
Nodes in HTML are sorted by node.id for consistent output ordering

data_access.rationale: Reads from webprobe.__version__ module attribute and writes JSON/HTML report files to run_dir

side_effects.rationale: Primary output phase that persists webprobe run results to disk for user consumption
